reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Real transformers.js inference in a dedicated module Worker (models/toxicity-detection/worker.js), and deliberately LOW-LEVEL: AutoTokenizer + AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained("Xenova/toxic-bert",{dtype:"q8",device:"wasm"}), then tokenizer(text) -> model(inputs) -> logits.data. It reads the 6 raw independent logits and applies its own sigmoid() per label (multi-label, not softmax) so the "see inside" surface can show BOTH the raw logit and the sigmoid probability. WASM demo (no WebGPU), runs everywhere. Conformance calls-real-inference (source, matches classifier) + loads-clean tested-pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. Latency/scores NOT fabricated in this backfill.
rubric.notes: Labels come from model.config.id2label (toxic, severe_toxic, obscene, threat, insult, identity_hate) — not hardcoded. Batch triage + a moderation gate reuse the same scoreText path.

rubric.evidence: Expected output is 6 per-label {label, logit, score=sigmoid(logit)}, sorted high->low, each independent (a comment can be high on several at once; scores don't sum to 1). The page renders per-label probability bars (toxicity.js renderLabelBars: a bar-track role=meter with aria-valuemin/max/now + aria-label '<label>: <pct> percent', flagged rows at/above the threshold) and a logit->sigmoid table. This IS the accessible probability-bar surface.
rubric.notes: The verdict (FLAG if any label >= threshold, else ALLOW) is derived from the real scores.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/toxic-bert, linked) as ONNX of unitary/toxic-bert (linked), task (text-classification, multi-label per-label sigmoid), the 6 labels, architecture (BERT-base fine-tuned on Jigsaw Toxic Comment), quantisation (q8), backend (WebAssembly, no WebGPU), download (~110 MB), license Apache-2.0. Matches worker.js. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation + declares-backend tested-pass.

Loads via shared createModelLoader (modelId:"Xenova/toxic-bert", dtype:"q8", sizeMB:110, requiresWebGPU:false). Valid cached model auto-initialises with no Load click; Download/Re-download/Update + clear-cache otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source) tested-pass; auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: env.allowLocalModels=false in the worker; modelId keyed on the repo so the ~110 MB cache is tracked for the no-silent-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence:
Status is

(runStatus); the bars container is aria-live; the verdict row toggles hidden when input is empty. On failure the worker posts {type:error} surfaced on the page. Conformance live-status-region + errors-on-the-page tested-pass; runs-without-webgpu-fallback asserted; honest-progress-retry-offline manual.

Every control is real + keyboard-operable: the text textarea, a threshold range slider (0.05–0.95, default 0.5) that re-computes which labels are flagged and the ALLOW/FLAG verdict live, and (on other rungs) a batch triage input. The threshold genuinely turns the numbers into a decision.
No hardcoded scores: every bar + the logit/sigmoid table + the verdict come from the real forward pass. Conformance no-fake-output manual-evidenced.

Strong: each probability bar is a role=meter with aria-valuemin/valuemax/aria-valuenow + a descriptive aria-label ('<label>: <pct> percent, flagged'), so magnitudes are readable non-visually. Status uses role=status aria-live; buttons have text names (buttons-have-names tested-pass); no content images (images-have-alt vacuously pass); light+dark WCAG-AA.
rubric.notes: This is the reference accessible-meter pattern the other classifier bars should follow.

rubric.evidence: Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are the tested assertions; input + bars + table stack fluidly, bars are %-based, the logit table scrolls if needed. parity-both-classes manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: WASM demo — fully usable on mobile; ~110 MB download surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Scoring runs in the dedicated module Worker; the main thread only renders 6 bar rows + a small table. Batch triage loops texts off-thread and reports as one job. Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source: new Worker) tested-pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low run heavy work off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread and break up long tasks so the browser can service input and repaint; workers + yielding keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tokenise + forward + sigmoid (and batch triage / gate) run in a dedicated module Worker; the page thread only renders the accessible bars + table.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker(type:module); conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates, accessible names for controls, loading state
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; semantic HTML + visible focus; mark language for correct shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — each probability bar is role=meter with aria-valuenow + a descriptive aria-label; status is role=status aria-live; the threshold + verdict are announced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: toxicity.js renderLabelBars role=meter aria-valuenow; conformance buttons-have-names + live-status-region pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form controls: range slider and text input labelling, keyboard operable
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate every input with a label, keep controls keyboard-operable, and reflect state changes accessibly.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the threshold range slider is a labelled native control that live-recomputes the flagged labels and the ALLOW/FLAG verdict.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: native <input type=range> re-renders verdict + bar flags

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow using flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap, intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — input, meter bars and the logit/sigmoid table stack fluidly with %-based widths; no horizontal overflow at mobile.

openQuestions: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached are manual-evidenced — a headless WASM run could capture the real 6-label logits/sigmoids + latency to flip them.
The moderation gate composes into multi-model chains (block toxic input before it reaches another model) — is that composition surfaced clearly enough as a reusable safety primitive?

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a real headless WASM scoring run (6 logits + sigmoids + latency) to satisfy io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached.
Document the toxic-bert moderation gate as a reusable pre-filter primitive other demo chains can adopt.

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique for toxicity-detection (Xenova/toxic-bert, ONNX of unitary/toxic-bert, text-classification MULTI-LABEL, q8, WASM — runs everywhere, ~110 MB, Apache-2.0). BERT-base Jigsaw moderation head run LOW-LEVEL (tokenizer -> model -> raw logits -> per-label sigmoid) in a dedicated module Worker, exposing both the logit and the probability for 6 independent labels as accessible role=meter probability bars with a live threshold and ALLOW/FLAG verdict; batch triage + a moderation gate reuse the same path. No WebGPU needed. No fabricated scores; latency NOT invented in this backfill. Accessibility is the batch's strongest (role=meter/aria-valuenow reference pattern). guidanceConsulted: break-up-long-tasks, accessibility, forms, css-layout. Weakness flagged: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
